input.policy_issue: Comprehensive reform package (subsidy cuts + welfare expansion + procedural override) submitted as a confidence motion
input.party_order: Pass the package as-is; do not split it.
input.faction_order: Force a public vote; reject committee referral.
input.district_pressure: Supporters want visible action, not another delay; opponents warn of overreach.
input.media_pressure: Media frames him as a strongman eroding parliamentary checks.
input.player_instruction: Pass the reform without splitting the package, but avoid giving opponents a clean 'he broke the system' story.
recent_memory: The player previously vouched for a district chair who later abstained.

expected_output.public_statement: The package moves together, and it moves in daylight. Let every member vote where the country can see them.
expected_output.private_reason: He refuses to split the welfare expansion from the subsidy cuts — splitting would lose the lever that binds the people to him and leave only the part that makes enemies. A public confidence vote turns a buried committee death into a named, visible choice, which is exactly the kind of bold-reset board he plays best.
memory_write: The player urged him to pass the package whole during confidence_vote_001; he forced a public confidence vote and kept the welfare expansion intact.
